result:
CORS errors happen when your frontend (origin A) makes a request to a server (origin B) and the server doesn't send back the `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header permitting origin A. The browser blocks the response — the request usually *did* reach the server.

Common causes:
- Server doesn't set `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` (or sets it to a different origin)
- Preflight `OPTIONS` request fails (missing `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` / `-Headers`)
- Using `credentials: 'include'` but server returns `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` (must be an exact origin + `Allow-Credentials: true`)
- Hitting `http://` from an `https://` page, or a typo in the URL

To diagnose, paste the exact error text and the Network tab entry for the failing request (status, request/response headers). That tells us which of the above it is.

Common causes:
- Server doesn't set `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` (or sets it to a different origin)
- Preflight `OPTIONS` request fails (missing `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` / `-Headers`)
- Using `credentials: 'include'` but server returns `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` (must be an exact origin + `Allow-Credentials: true`)
- Hitting `http://` from an `https://` page, or a typo in the URL

To diagnose, paste the exact error text and the Network tab entry for the failing request (status, request/response headers). That tells us which of the above it is.
